name: Architecture Layer Extractor
short_description: Builds a module-boundary and import graph for a multi-module Kotlin/Swift codebase, then validates that dependencies respect declared architectural layer rules (domain → data → presentation, KMP shared vs platform-specific). Flags illegal cross-layer imports and cycles.
tech_stack: tree-sitter (tree-sitter-kotlin v0.3.x, tree-sitter/tree-sitter-swift) — AST import declaration extraction
modules-graph-assert (jraska, v2.9.0, Jun 2025) — Gradle-side rule assertion, cycle detection, max-height rules
iurysza/module-graph (v1.x, Oct 2025) — Mermaid/DOT visualisation layer exported to .knowledge/ markdown
ArchUnit (TNG, v1.x) — JVM bytecode layer-rule enforcement in Kotlin modules as test suite
SPM package manifest parser (Swift) — Package.swift dependency declarations for Swift module graph
custom Python driver — orchestrates tree-sitter queries, emits JSON node/edge stream to Graphiti
entity_model: Produces :Module nodes (name, kind=[domain|data|presentation|shared|platform], path, build_system=[gradle|spm], target=[android|ios|common]) and :DEPENDS_ON edges (from_module, to_module, declared_config=[api|implementation|compileOnly|regular], is_illegal=bool). Produces :ImportViolation nodes (source_module, target_module, violation_rule, file_path, line) when a layer rule is breached. :Module applies structural/capability facets: structural=layer-boundary, capability=LayerEnforcement. Cycle findings produce :ArchCycle nodes referencing a list of :Module ids. KMP shared/platform split modelled as kind=shared or kind=platform with a :KMPSplit edge linking shared → platform counterparts.
storage_format: hybrid — primary graph-node writes to Graphiti (Module, DEPENDS_ON, ImportViolation, ArchCycle), plus a snapshot module-graph.md written to .knowledge/ for human review and LLM prompt injection. Rationale: graph-node enables downstream Cypher queries; markdown enables stable snapshot sharing with non-graph agents.

prior_art.name: modules-graph-assert
prior_art.description: Gradle plugin for asserting module graph rules (layer types, height, cycles). v2.9.0 released Jun 2025, Kotlin 2.0 support. MIT license. KMP support partial — Issue #249 open as of 2024; commonMain dependencies not recognised.

prior_art.name: iurysza/module-graph
prior_art.description: Gradle plugin generating Mermaid module dependency graph, embeds into README. Oct 2025 release. Visualisation only, no rule enforcement.

prior_art.name: ArchUnit
prior_art.description: JVM bytecode architecture rule library. Kotlin-supported via bytecode analysis. Layer rules expressed as JUnit tests. No native Swift/KMP awareness. Apache 2.0.

prior_art.name: tree-sitter-kotlin (fwcd)
prior_art.description: Kotlin grammar for tree-sitter, validated against JetBrains PSI parser. Used by Neovim, Helix, GitHub Linguist. Supports import declarations.

prior_art.name: tree-sitter/tree-sitter-swift
prior_art.description: Official tree-sitter Swift grammar, maintained under tree-sitter org. Jun 2025 activity confirmed.

prior_art.name: ENRE-java (incremental)
prior_art.description: Entity/dependency extractor for Java with incremental analysis (Nov 2024). Not Swift/Kotlin-native but architectural approach is directly applicable.

prior_art.name: RepoGraph (arXiv 2410.14684)
prior_art.description: 2024 paper describing repo-level code graph enhanced AI SE. Import-graph construction technique aligns with this extractor's approach.

graphiti_episode_type: EntityNode — :Module nodes are permanent structural entities; :DEPENDS_ON edges are permanent unless a module is removed (carry end_time on deletion); :ImportViolation edges carry end_time once the violation is fixed.
ingest_perf_est: [uncertain] Full ingest on 20-library KMP repo (~200K LOC Kotlin + ~150K LOC Swift): estimated 3–8 minutes using tree-sitter parsing (fast) plus Gradle task execution (modules-graph-assert assertModuleGraph). Incremental: estimated 30–90 seconds per changed module (re-parse affected files + re-run assertions). Based on analogy with tree-sitter benchmark data and modules-graph-assert task overhead, not direct measurement.
failure_modes: modules-graph-assert does not recognise KMP commonMain dependencies (Issue #249, open 2024) — KMP shared modules may show false-negative missing-dependency violations
ArchUnit operates on JVM bytecode only — Swift modules require separate tree-sitter-based rule enforcement pipeline
tree-sitter-kotlin parse errors on advanced Kotlin 2.x syntax (context receivers, multi-dollar interpolation) may silently miss import nodes
Generated code (Kotlin code-gen by KSP, Swift code-gen by SKIE) imports phantom modules that pollute the layer graph with noise
Monorepo SPM packages with platform conditionals (#if canImport(UIKit)) not captured by manifest-level parsing; import graph may be under-complete for iOS-only modules
open_questions: How should KMP expect/actual split be represented in the layer graph — two :Module nodes (shared + platform) or one node with a platform attribute? Resolving this requires coordination with Item 4 (KMP Platform-Bridge Extractor) to avoid duplicate module nodes.
modules-graph-assert KMP issue (#249) — should Gimle-Palace implement a custom Gradle task that walks KMP source sets, or wait for upstream fix? Blocks deterministic enforcement for KMP modules.

ArchUnit requires JUnit test runner — is running architecture tests as part of the ingest pipeline acceptable, or should tree-sitter-based rule checking replace ArchUnit entirely to avoid JVM test-runner dependency?
How to handle Gradle configuration-cache compatibility for modules-graph-assert (Issue #120)? Without CC support, incremental Gradle builds are significantly slowed.

Layer rule schema: where is the authoritative source for layer definitions (YAML config, build.gradle.kts annotations, or inferred from package names)? Inferred-from-package-names approach is fragile for non-standard codebases like Unstoppable Wallet.
adversarial_resistance: high — import declarations are syntactic and not obfuscatable at source level; generated code is a noise source but not an adversarial scenario. Kotlin macros (KSP) produce new files that are parseable by tree-sitter.
